vulnerability.title: Set Boot Loader Password in grub2
vulnerability.description: The grub2 boot loader should have a superuser account and password protection enabled to protect boot-time settings. Since plaintext passwords are a security risk, generate a hash for the password by running the following command: # grub2-setpassword When prompted, enter the password that was selected.
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Set Boot Loader Password in grub2

triage_decision.reason: The grub2 boot loader should have a superuser account and password protection enabled to protect boot-time settings. Since plaintext passwords are a security risk, a hash for the password should be generated by running grub2-setpassword and prompting for the password.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; CWE-259: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

review_feedback: The proposed remediation plan is empty; no actions are specified to set the GRUB2 password, so it will not mitigate the vulnerability.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed remediation plan is empty; no actions are specified to set the GRUB2 password, so it will not mitigate the vulnerability.
concerns: Plan does not include running grub2-setpassword or configuring GRUB.
suggested_improvements: Run grub2-setpassword and follow prompts to set a GRUB superuser password.
Ensure the password hash is stored securely.
